ingredients.item: 1 Add the coffee and sugar to a Turkish coffeepot.
ingredients.original: 1 Add the coffee and sugar to a Turkish coffeepot.

ingredients.item: 2 Using a small Turkish coffee cup, measure out 5 cups (1.2 L) of filtered water and add it to the coffeepot.
ingredients.original: 2 Using a small Turkish coffee cup, measure out 5 cups (1.2 L) of filtered water and add it to the coffeepot.

ingredients.item: 3 Place the coffeepot over medium heat and stir for a minute to dissolve the coffee and sugar. Let the coffee come to a boil, 4 to 5 minutes. As soon as the coffee starts to boil, remove the pot from the heat. A layer of coffee foam will form at the top of the pot. Using a teaspoon, scoop the foam into each coffee cup, then pour the coffee. Each cup will have a small layer of foam. The bottom of the coffee will have a thick layer of coffee grounds which you leave in the pot (but it’s fine if some of it gets into the cup).
ingredients.original: 3 Place the coffeepot over medium heat and stir for a minute to dissolve the coffee and sugar. Let the coffee come to a boil, 4 to 5 minutes. As soon as the coffee starts to boil, remove the pot from the heat. A layer of coffee foam will form at the top of the pot. Using a teaspoon, scoop the foam into each coffee cup, then pour the coffee. Each cup will have a small layer of foam. The bottom of the coffee will have a thick layer of coffee grounds which you leave in the pot (but it’s fine if some of it gets into the cup).

ingredients.item: 4 Serve with Turkish delight, cookies, chocolates, or any other sweet treat.
ingredients.original: 4 Serve with Turkish delight, cookies, chocolates, or any other sweet treat.
